rulings.Case Title: NAVISTAR, INC. VS. CALIFORNIA AIR RESOURCES BOARD, AN AGENCY OF THE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reconsider Ruling Motion For Leave To File Complaint In Internvention; Notice Of Lodgemnt
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 22, 2011, line 6. PLAINTIFF NAVISTAR, INC.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reconsider Ruling, Motion For Leave To File Complaint In Internvention. Plaintiff¿s Motion for reconsideration of the Court¿s Order granting EMA leave to intervene is denied. First, Plaintiff¿s reliance on the deposition testimony of EMA President Mandel is misplaced. Plaintiff alleges that Mr. Mandel admitted that EMA has no direct or immediate interesting this case. However, this allegation is taken out of context. Mr. Mandel in fact did testify to the implication of this case¿s adjudication on other non-road engine manufacturers. Therefore, there is no new matter presented to warrant reversal of the Court¿s original order. Plaintiff¿s additional argument that EMA violated its internal rules by not polling every member of the EMA before it participated in this lawsuit is also without merit. Mr. Mandel¿s deposition transcript indicates that 27 of the 28 members were balloted. The only member it did not ballot was an employee of Plaintiff serving as a board member. According to EMA rules it was proper to exclude this member who had a conflict of interest in this vote. The Court finds that EMA did not agree to withdrawl from the litigation in that the parties did not reach a stipulation. = (302/LMG)
